description_long: This broom features a long, slender wooden handle with alternating light and dark bands, giving it a visually striking, striped appearance. The handle is straight and appears to be made from a single piece of wood, with no visible ergonomic enhancements or grip textures. The bristle section is not visible in any of the provided views, suggesting the asset may be missing the typical sweeping head or it is obscured. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, with no visible attachments, hooks, or mounting features. The surface finish appears smooth and the object is free from visible wear, damage, or dirt. There are no visible brand labels or manufacturer markings on any part of the broom.
description: A striped wooden broom handle with alternating light and dark bands, straight and smooth, with no visible bristles or brand markings.
description_view_0: A side view showing the broom's thin profile and the alternating light and dark bands along the handle.
description_view_1: A top-down view highlighting the flat, rectangular cross-section and the striped pattern along the length.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 0, showing the opposite side of the handle with the same striped pattern.
description_view_3: A frontal view displaying the full length of the handle and the striped design, with no visible bristle section.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, round wooden handle with alternating light and dark bands, no ergonomic features or grip enhancements, no visible end caps or attachments.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: Bristle section not visible; no bristles apparent in any view.
attribute_mounting_position: No dedicated mounting feature visible; likely intended to be leaned vertically or stored horizontally.
